bug_report.summary: Status bar word/character counts differ after code change for the same document state
steps_to_reproduce: Open the application (same initial state/tutorial file visible).
Observe the bottom status bar statistics for the left editor pane (word and character counts).
Dismiss the update notification dialog (click “Not now”).
expected_behaviors: For the same document content and UI state, word and character counts should match previous behavior (no change introduced by link/image parser refactor).
actual_behaviors: Word count and character count values are lower in the after-change build (e.g., ~1,596 words / 9,023 characters before vs ~1,573 words / 8,897 characters after).

bug_report.summary: Word/character count calculation changed for Markdown link with title (counts include URL/title in after-change build)
steps_to_reproduce: Create a new Markdown file (defaults) so it opens in an editor pane.
Check the word/character counts for that new file (top-right word indicator and/or the pane’s bottom status bar).
expected_behaviors: Word/character counts should remain consistent with pre-change behavior for Markdown links (e.g., counting the visible link text similarly to before).
actual_behaviors: Before-change build shows ~1 word / 7 characters (consistent with counting only “Example”).
After-change build shows a much larger count (e.g., ~6 words / 42 characters), indicating the URL/title (or other non-visible link parts) are now included in stats.

bug_report.summary: Right-click on the outer text portion no longer shows link context menu (outer portion not recognized as link)
steps_to_reproduce: Right-click on the outer text portion (e.g., on “Outer”, not on “Inner”).
expected_behaviors: A link-specific context menu should appear for the outer link region, exposing link actions (Open/Copy/Remove link) and showing the outer URL (https://example.com/outer).
actual_behaviors: After the change, right-clicking the outer text portion shows the generic editor context menu (Bold/Italic/Insert link/etc.) instead of a link context menu, implying the outer portion is not treated as a link.

bug_report.summary: Reference-style link renders with visible bracket/reference markup in preview (incorrect link text rendering)
steps_to_reproduce: `[You can use numbers for reference-style link definitions][1]`
Observe the rendered preview of the first line.
expected_behaviors: The rendered link text should display as: “You can use numbers for reference-style link definitions” (clickable), without showing the surrounding `[` `]` or the reference id `[1]` in the visible text.
actual_behaviors: After the change, the rendered output shows the reference-style link with visible bracket/reference markup in the displayed text (the preview appears to include literal `[` / `]` and/or reference markers), instead of rendering only the clean link label.
